findings.title: BC baseline (Fig. 3) has neither training code nor data in the repo
findings.quote:
## Countdown

1. Download the SFT and RL datasets
findings.claim: The Countdown section of the README and all recipe scripts provide only SoS (base_sft), ReST, and Guided-ReST pipelines; there is no behavior-cloning (BC) data file on HuggingFace and no BC training/eval script, yet Fig. 3 and the abstract report a BC curve ("BC achieves less than 40% accuracy").
findings.concern: A reported baseline that establishes the lower bound of the comparison cannot be reproduced because neither the BC training data (optimal-solution-only traces) nor a BC training script is provided.
findings.resolution: Authors: please add the BC data-preparation script (converting extra_info.solution into solution-only SFT targets) and the BC training command.
findings.reason: README lines 26-90 cover only SoS/ReST/Guided-ReST pipelines; no BC script or data-prep file was found anywhere in recipe/countdown/, confirming the baseline is absent.

findings.title: Token-level MDP ablation (Fig. 5) has no corresponding script
findings.claim: All three RL drivers (base/run_rl.sh, rest/run_rl.sh, guided_rest/run_rl.sh) hardcode the operation-level formulation via `algorithm.adv_estimator=hgae` and `actor_rollout_ref.actor.policy_loss.loss_mode=hppo`; no script runs standard token-level PPO (e.g. `adv_estimator=gae`, default loss).
findings.concern: Figure 5 compares the proposed operation-level MDP against a token-level MDP and reports a 83%→87% gain, but the token-level configuration that produces the 83% baseline curve is not provided, so the ablation cannot be reproduced.
findings.resolution: Authors: please add the token-level-MDP RL script (the exact adv_estimator/loss_mode/config used for the "Ours (token-level)" curve in Fig. 5).
findings.reason: run_rl.sh line 9 confirms `algorithm.adv_estimator=hgae` is hardcoded; a search across all countdown shell scripts found no script using `adv_estimator=gae` or any token-level PPO configuration, confirming the ablation script is absent.

findings.title: Dense subgoal-reward RL ablation (Fig. 6 / §5.5) has no code
findings.claim: The only reward function is the binary outcome reward (1 for success, 0 otherwise); a grep across the repo for the subgoal reward (value 0.25, "subgoal", "dense") returns nothing in recipe/countdown.
findings.concern: §5.5 / Fig. 6 report a "PPO (dense)" experiment using a subgoal-based reward R_subgoal=0.25 combined with the outcome reward, but no code computes this dense reward, so the ablation cannot be reproduced.
findings.resolution: Authors: please add the subgoal/dense reward function and the RL script used for the "PPO (dense)" curve in Fig. 6.
findings.reason: reward_function.py lines 20-27 exactly match the quoted binary reward; a grep for '0.25', 'subgoal.*reward', and 'dense' across recipe/countdown/ returned no reward-function results, confirming the dense subgoal reward is absent.

findings.title: SoS heuristic DFS/BFS trace-generation code not in repo (data only on HF)
findings.quote:
def main():
    # download sft dataset
    sft_train_dataset = load_dataset("symoon11/countdown-sft", split="train")
    sft_valid_dataset = load_dataset("symoon11/countdown-sft", split="validation")

    # save sft dataset
    sft_train_dataset.to_parquet(f"data/countdown/sft/train.parquet")
    sft_valid_dataset.to_parquet(f"data/countdown/sft/valid.parquet")
findings.claim: The SoS base-SFT search traces are obtained only by downloading the pre-built `symoon11/countdown-sft` dataset; the symbolic heuristic-guided DFS/BFS generator that produced these traces (paper §5.1.1, "we generate search traces using heuristic-guided DFS and BFS over 500K training examples") is not present in the repo.
findings.concern: The SoS baseline's data-generation procedure is not reproducible from the code; only the resulting dataset is distributed, so the trace-construction step cannot be inspected or re-run.
findings.resolution: Authors: please add (or link) the DFS/BFS search-trace generator used to build the countdown-sft dataset, or confirm it is intentionally distributed as data only.
findings.reason: download_data.py lines 18-25 exactly match the quoted code; the function only downloads the pre-built HuggingFace dataset and no DFS/BFS generator file exists in the repo, confirming the trace-generation step is absent.

findings.title: Table 1 preliminary partial-solution experiment has no code
findings.quote: "Table 1: Accuracy and cross-entropy loss of search traces from partial solutions of different lengths."
findings.claim: Table 1 (accuracy and cross-entropy loss of traces seeded from partial optimal solutions of length 0/1/2/3, over 10K examples) is a motivating experiment, but no script in the repo seeds generation from fixed-length partial solutions or computes the cross-entropy loss reported there.
findings.concern: The motivating numbers in Table 1 cannot be reproduced from the released code.
findings.resolution: Authors: please add the script that prepends partial solutions of length t and measures continuation accuracy and CE loss (Table 1).
findings.reason: This is an absence claim about the paper's motivating experiment; no script computing CE loss or seeding from fixed-length partial solutions was found in the repo, and the paper.pdf quote is a direct table caption — consistent with a missing reproduction script.

findings.title: code-repair download_model.py writes to qwen.2.5_7b/ but scripts read qwen2.5_7b/
findings.quote:
model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained("Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct", dtype=torch.bfloat16)
model.save_pretrained("checkpoints/code_repair/qwen.2.5_7b/huggingface")

# Download the tokenizer
tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained("Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct")
tokenizer.save_pretrained("checkpoints/code_repair/qwen.2.5_7b/huggingface")
findings.claim: download_model.py saves the base model/tokenizer to `checkpoints/code_repair/qwen.2.5_7b/huggingface` (with a dot in `qwen.2.5_7b`), whereas the README (`model_name=qwen2.5_7b`) and every training/gen script read `checkpoints/code_repair/qwen2.5_7b/huggingface` (no dot, e.g. run_sft_1.sh:5 `model_path=checkpoints/code_repair/qwen2.5_7b/huggingface`).
findings.concern: Following the README verbatim downloads the base model into a directory that the subsequent generation and SFT scripts never look in, so the code-repair pipeline fails to find the base model out of the box.
findings.resolution: Rename the save path to `checkpoints/code_repair/qwen2.5_7b/huggingface` (remove the dot) so it matches the scripts and README.
findings.reason: download_model.py lines 23 and 27 save to `qwen.2.5_7b/huggingface` (with dot), while all SFT scripts (e.g. guided_rest/run_sft_1.sh:5, rest/run_sft_1.sh:5) consistently read from `qwen2.5_7b/huggingface` (no dot), confirming the path mismatch.

findings.title: Code-repair SFT computes loss on all assistant turns, paper says only the last
findings.quote:
i = 0
while i < len(messages):
    cur_messages = messages[i]
    if cur_messages["role"] == "assistant":
        # Process assistant message
        tokens, loss_mask, attention_mask = self._process_message_tokens(
            messages,
            i,
            i + 1,
            is_assistant=True,
            continue_final_message=continue_final_message and i == len(messages) - 1,
            enable_thinking=enable_thinking,
            tools=tools,
        )
findings.claim: verl's multi-turn SFT dataset assigns loss_mask=1 to every assistant message (the loop tokenizes each assistant turn with is_assistant=True; per-message override at lines 319-326 is only used if the message dict carries a `loss_mask` key). recipe/code_repair/main_data.py builds `messages = prompt + response` with no per-message loss_mask, and main_gen.py creates assistant messages without one, so all assistant turns in a multi-turn episode are trained on.
findings.concern: The paper (§5.1.2) states "We compute the loss only on the last model response, following the practice of Snell et al.", but the released code-repair SFT trains on every assistant turn of the episode, a different training objective than reported.
findings.resolution: Authors: confirm whether the code-repair SFT data carries a per-turn loss_mask (set to 0 on non-final assistant turns); if not, reconcile the code with the "loss only on last response" claim.
findings.reason: multiturn_sft_dataset.py lines 283-326 confirm every assistant turn gets is_assistant=True (loss_mask=1) and the per-message override (lines 319-326) only fires if the message dict has a 'loss_mask' key; a grep of recipe/code_repair/ shows no per-turn loss_mask is set, so all assistant turns are trained on, contradicting the paper's claim of loss on the last response only.
